description: Personal portfolio website for Bianca Tomoe Sato — UI/UX & Brand Designer. Single-page scrolling site built with Next.js (App Router) and Tailwind CSS. Light editorial aesthetic inspired by modern design agency sites with warm neutral tones, elegant serif typography, and generous whitespace. All content hardcoded via JSON files.

designDirection.aesthetic: Light editorial — warm, clean, magazine-like. NOT dark mode. Inspired by modern design agency portfolios with organic photography, warm neutrals, and confident serif typography.

typography.display: Playfair Display — elegant serif for headings, hero name, and section titles. Used at large scale with italic variant for emphasis.
typography.body: DM Sans — clean geometric sans-serif for body text, nav, buttons, and labels.
typography.accent: DM Sans italic or Playfair Display italic — for taglines, quotes, and decorative text.

designPrinciples: Light and warm — cream/off-white backgrounds, dark text, warm neutral accents
Editorial layout — asymmetric grids, mixed-size columns, text alongside large imagery
Typography-driven — oversized serif headings, strong hierarchy, mixed serif/sans pairing
Generous whitespace — sections breathe, nothing cramped, gallery-like spacing
Interactive scroll animations — scroll-reactive transitions between sections via Framer Motion, with smooth parallax and staggered reveals
Photography-forward — large image placeholders integrated into the layout, not afterthoughts
Trust signals — trusted-by logos section, process breakdown, stats/metrics display

hero.json.name: Bianca Tomoe Sato
hero.json.description: Transforming ideas into stunning visuals — UI/UX and brand design that captivates, engages, and delivers results.

highlights.label: User-Centered Design
highlights.description: Intuitive interfaces that put users first

highlights.label: Brand Identity & Strategy
highlights.description: Visual systems that tell your story

about.json.headline: Crafting Meaningful Brands & Intuitive Experiences
bio: Hey, I'm Bian, a UI/UX and brand designer passionate about creating visually compelling and user-friendly digital experiences.

With a keen eye for aesthetics and a deep understanding of user behavior, I design brands and interfaces that not only look great but also resonate with audiences. Whether it's building a brand identity from scratch, designing a digital product for mobile, or refining an existing design — I blend strategy, creativity, and functionality to bring ideas to life.
Let's collaborate and make something extraordinary.

process.json.heading: A Streamlined Process for Exceptional Design

steps.title: Discover & Research
steps.description: Deep dive into your brand, audience, and goals to establish a strong foundation.

steps.title: Design & Prototype
steps.description: Crafting wireframes, visual concepts, mockups, and interactive prototypes.

steps.title: Refine & Deliver
steps.description: Iterating based on feedback for a polished, production-ready final design.

definitions: Substantivo feminino que se refere a uma unidade de linguagem que possui significado e é composta por um ou mais fonemas.
São usadas para formar frases e expressar ideias e sentimentos. Podem ser classificadas em diferentes categorias.
1. Forte afeição por outra pessoa, nascida de laços de consanguinidade ou de relações sociais.
2. Atração baseada no desejo sexual.
1. estado de quem se acha ou se sente desacompanhado ou só; isolamento.
2. caráter dos locais ermos, solitários.
1. sentimento de tristeza profunda pela morte de alguém.
2. tempo durante o qual devem manifestar-se certos sinais do luto.
3. conjunto de sinais externos que os costumes associam à perda de parente próximo ou pessoa querida.
Alteração ou modificação do estado normal de algo;
modificação ou transferência de alguma coisa para um outro lugar;
alteração ou substituição de uma pessoa ou coisa por outra.
alavanca com a qual se realiza a troca das marchas num automóvel.
1. Ato ou efeito de motivar.
1. Ato ou efeito de motivar, exposição de motivos ou causas, conjunto de fatores, os quais agem entre si e determinam a conduta de um indivíduo.
[Física] Estado do sistema cujas forças que sobre ele agem se contrabalançam e se anulam de maneira análoga;
posição do corpo que se encontra estável (desprovido de oscilação ou desvio);
força que age de maneira igual entre duas ou mais coisas (ou pessoas);
cujas quantidades se apresentam de modo igual, proporcional;
em estado aquilo que permanece estável (constante);
estabilidade; equilíbrio de oportunidades de trabalho;
em que há harmonia; que se distribui de maneira proporcional;
equilíbrio de uma estátua; que apresenta comedimento emocional;
cujo estado mental é estável; autocontrole.

bullets: é uma concepção que fala da contemplação do que é imperfeito e assimétrios.

definitions: Conhecimento de si próprio, das suas características, qualidades, imperfeições, sentimentos etc.; que caracterizam o indivíduos por si próprio.

items.description: Designing brand identities and digital interfaces for startups and small businesses.
Studying Design with focus on visual communication, branding, and digital product design.

pricing.json.heading: Choose Your Plan
pricing.json.description: Creative solutions to fit every project need — from branding to complete digital presence.

plans.name: Pro
plans.subtitle: Growth Package
features: UI/UX design for mobile or web

items.question: What services do you offer?
items.answer: I specialize in UI/UX design, brand identity, visual design, and digital product design.

items.question: How long does a typical project take?
items.answer: Depending on the scope, most projects take between 2-6 weeks from kickoff to final delivery.

items.question: Do you work with startups or established brands?
items.answer: Both! I love working with startups building from scratch and established brands looking to refresh.

items.question: What does your design process look like?
items.answer: I follow a structured process: discover & research, design & prototype, then refine & deliver. Collaboration is at the center of every step.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and layout
userStories.description: Set up Next.js project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, responsive layout shell with navbar, footer, and smooth scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js app created with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS configured
Custom theme tokens applied: warm neutral palette (--bg-primary: #F5F0EB, --text-primary: #1A1A1A, --accent: #1A1A1A), Playfair Display + DM Sans fonts loaded via next/font
Fixed navbar: logo/name on left, nav links on right (Home, About, Portfolio, Pricing, Contact), dark CTA button 'MENU' or hamburger on mobile
Navbar background is transparent, becomes semi-transparent with backdrop-blur on scroll
Footer with copyright, social links (Behance, LinkedIn, Instagram), and sitemap-style link columns
Mobile hamburger menu with smooth animation
Smooth scroll behavior enabled globally (html { scroll-behavior: smooth })
Semantic HTML throughout (header, main, section, footer)
Static export configured (output: 'export' in next.config.js)

userStories.title: Hero section
userStories.description: Full-viewport hero with editorial layout: greeting text, large serif name/title, description, CTA button, and 4 highlight cards below. Asymmetric layout with profile photo placeholder on right.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-viewport hero section (min-h-screen) with --bg-primary (#F5F0EB) background
Left column: greeting 'Hey, I'm Bian,' in DM Sans, then 'A UI/UX & Brand DESIGNER' in large Playfair Display with '& Brand' in italic
Description paragraph below title in --text-secondary
Dark CTA button ('Contact me') with rounded corners
Right side: large profile photo placeholder (rounded corners, warm-toned border)
Below hero: 4 highlight cards in a row (User-Centered Design, Brand Identity & Strategy, Responsive & Modern UI, Seamless Prototyping) with icon and short description
Content sourced from data/hero.json
Staggered fade-in animation on page load (CSS only, using animation-delay)
Responsive: stacks vertically on mobile

acceptanceCriteria: Section heading 'Crafting Meaningful Brands & Intuitive Experiences' in large Playfair Display
Bio paragraphs in DM Sans, --text-secondary color
Right side: grid of 6-8 placeholder images in varied sizes (masonry-like or structured grid)
Content sourced from data/about.json
Generous whitespace between text and images
Fade-in on scroll (CSS only with Intersection Observer trigger)

Dark CTA button below stats

Project cards stacked vertically: each has a large image placeholder (16:9 or wider), dark overlay on hover
Below image: project title in Playfair Display, description in DM Sans, tag pills

Links to live site and/or Behance for each project

Visual connector line or border between entries in --border color
Most recent entry first

Fade-in on scroll for each entry

Question in DM Sans bold, answer in regular weight on expand

Form fields: Name, Email, Message — styled with warm neutral theme (--bg-secondary background, --border borders)
Focus state: darker border or subtle accent
Client-side validation: all required, valid email format
Submit button in dark accent (--accent: #1A1A1A) with hover state
Success message shown inline on submit (no backend, use mailto or static handler)
Social links alongside form (Behance, LinkedIn, Instagram, email)
Responsive: form and links stack on mobile

Meta description set
Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image) configured
All images use Next.js Image component
Lighthouse performance score >= 90 on desktop

userStories.title: Placeholder images with free-license stock photos
userStories.description: Replace all image placeholders throughout the site with real free-license images (Unsplash/Pexels) for visual testing and realistic preview of the final layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile photo in Hero section replaced with a real portrait-style image from Unsplash (e.g. https://images.unsplash.com — UTM params stripped, no attribution required under Unsplash License)
About section photo grid populated with 6–8 varied free-license images (mix of workspace, design process, lifestyle) from Unsplash or Pexels
Process section large image replaced with a relevant workspace or creative process photo from Unsplash
Project card for 'Soundwave' uses a dark/tech-aesthetic image from Unsplash (e.g. audio equipment, abstract tech)
Project card for 'Lumiglow' uses a beauty/skincare-aesthetic image from Unsplash (e.g. soft lighting, minimal product shot)
All images referenced via direct HTTPS URL (no local download required for testing) or downloaded to public/images/ with filename matching the existing JSON references
All images use Next.js <Image> component with appropriate width, height, and alt text
Images chosen to match the warm editorial aesthetic (neutral tones, soft lighting, no harsh filters)
No paid or rights-reserved images — only Unsplash License or CC0
A comment or README note documents the image sources for future reference

userStories.title: Audit and replace project images based on content relevance
userStories.description: Review all images currently in the project's public/images/ directory. For each image, evaluate whether it visually matches the section it's used in (hero portrait, about grid, process, portfolio cards). Replace images that don't fit the context — e.g. a generic stock photo used for a skincare brand project card, or an unrelated image in the about grid — with better-suited free-license alternatives. Keep images that already align well with their section's tone and subject matter.
acceptanceCriteria: All images in public/images/ are catalogued with their current usage location (hero, about grid, process, project cards, etc.)
Each image is evaluated for relevance: does the subject, color tone, and mood match the section's content and the warm editorial aesthetic?
Images that are clearly mismatched or generic placeholders are flagged for replacement
Images that already fit their context (correct subject matter, appropriate tone, good quality) are explicitly kept and documented as 'approved'

Replacement images for portfolio project cards must match the project theme — e.g. 'Soundwave' (audio/tech) gets a dark, tech-forward image; 'Lumiglow' (skincare/beauty) gets a soft, luxurious product image
Replacement images for the about section grid should reflect the designer's world: workspace shots, design tools, creative process, lifestyle — varied but cohesive
Replacement images for the hero section portrait should be a professional, warm-toned portrait that fits the editorial aesthetic
Replacement images for the process section should depict hands-on creative work (sketching, prototyping, design review)
All replacement images must be free-license (Unsplash License or CC0) with source URLs documented
Replaced images use the same filenames or the JSON data files are updated to reference new filenames
All images continue to use Next.js <Image> component with correct width, height, and descriptive alt text
A before/after log is produced listing: image filename, section used, verdict (keep/replace), and reason
The overall visual coherence of the site is maintained or improved — no jarring mismatches in color temperature or style between sections

userStories.title: Interactive scroll-driven animations with Framer Motion
userStories.description: Add scroll-reactive animations throughout the site using Framer Motion, focused on smooth transitions between sections. The goal is an interactive, engaging feel where content responds to the user's scroll position — parallax layers, staggered reveals, and fluid section entrances that make the single-page experience feel cinematic rather than static.
acceptanceCriteria: Framer Motion installed and configured as the animation library (supersedes previous CSS-only animation constraint in FR-9)
Each major section (Hero, About, Process, Portfolio, Skills, Experience, Pricing, FAQ, Contact) has a scroll-triggered entrance animation using Framer Motion's `whileInView`
Section entrances are varied — not every section uses the same animation. Mix of: fade-up, fade-in with scale, slide-in from left/right, staggered children reveals
Parallax depth effect on at least 2 sections: background elements or images move at a different speed than foreground content using `useScroll` + `useTransform`
Hero section: title lines animate in sequentially (staggered by ~0.15s each) with a subtle vertical slide + opacity transition on page load
About section: bio text fades in from left while the photo grid fades in from right, creating a split-reveal effect
Process section: each step (01, 02, 03) staggers in as the user scrolls, with the large image having a parallax offset
Portfolio project cards: each card scales subtly from 0.95 to 1 and fades in as it enters the viewport
Skills stats: numbers animate with a counting-up effect (e.g. 0% → 98%) when the section scrolls into view, triggered once
Smooth section-to-section flow: sections overlap slightly in their animation timing so there are no 'dead zones' while scrolling — content is always in motion or settling
All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`: if the user's OS has reduced motion enabled, animations are disabled or simplified to simple fades
Animations are performant: use `transform` and `opacity` only (GPU-composited properties), no layout-triggering animations. Target 60fps on mid-range devices
Animation durations between 0.4s–0.8s with easing curves that feel organic (e.g. easeOut, [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0]), not mechanical linear
No animation plays on elements that are already visible in the initial viewport on page load (above-the-fold content animates on load, not on scroll)
Scroll-triggered animations fire once (not re-trigger when scrolling back up), unless parallax-based
CSS transitions remain for hover states and micro-interactions (buttons, cards, links) — Framer Motion is used for scroll-driven and entrance animations only

userStories.title: Social Media Showcase component
userStories.description: Create a new SocialPosts section that renders 12 Instagram post cards for @psicologamarinahiga as HTML/CSS (not images) in a horizontal carousel showing 3 cards at a time. The cards mix Portuguese word definitions (cream background + burgundy sidebar) with Japanese philosophical concepts (burgundy background + light text). The component must be extensible — the JSON data uses a `type` discriminator field so future post types (quote, carousel, image, etc.) can be added without changing the component structure. Includes filter tabs (Todos / Português / 日本語), navigation arrows + dot indicators, project metadata footer (client, type, post count, link to Instagram), and staggered scroll animations via Framer Motion. This is a NEW standalone component — do NOT modify any existing components, data files, styles, or configs.
acceptanceCriteria: PREREQUISITE: Before writing any code, execute the Mandatory Change Audit (Step 4 in Ralph agent instructions). The project owner made manual changes after US-015 that are NOT in progress.txt. Audit the full project, document all unrecorded changes in progress.txt as a 'Manual Changes Audit (pre-US-016)' block, then proceed. Without this audit the agent will work with stale information.

New file: data/socialPosts.json containing sectionLabel, heading, description, client handle, and a posts array with all 12 posts. Each post has a `type` field ('portuguese' or 'japanese') used as discriminator for rendering. Portuguese posts have: id, type, word, pronunciation (nullable), classification, definitions array. Japanese posts have: id, type, characters, romanization, meaning (nullable), optional divider boolean, optional bullets array. No content hardcoded in the component — everything comes from the JSON.

New file: components/SocialPosts.tsx — a single 'use client' component. No separate sub-component files. All sub-components (PortugueseCard, JapaneseCard, FilterTabs, carousel controls, project metadata) defined inside this one file.

Section header: label 'SOCIAL MEDIA DESIGN' in DM Sans uppercase with wide letter-spacing (0.2em), heading 'Design para Redes Sociais' in Playfair Display with 'Redes Sociais' in italic, description paragraph mentioning @psicologamarinahiga in burgundy (#6B1A1A) font weight 500.

Filter tabs: row of pill buttons (Todos, Português, 日本語) that filter the visible posts by type. Active tab has burgundy (#6B1A1A) background with cream text. Inactive tabs have transparent background with border. Filters use React useState — no URL state. 'Todos' shows all 12 posts. When a filter changes, the carousel resets to the first slide.

CAROUSEL LAYOUT (replaces the previous grid approach): Display exactly 3 cards side by side at a time on desktop, 2 on tablet (md breakpoint), 1 on mobile. The carousel is a horizontal sliding container — NOT a CSS grid. Cards slide left/right as a group when navigating. Use CSS transform translateX for smooth sliding (GPU-composited, 60fps). Transition duration 0.5s with ease [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0]. Gap of 20px between visible cards.

Carousel navigation — LEFT/RIGHT ARROWS: Two circular arrow buttons (40px diameter) positioned at the vertical center of the carousel area, one on each side. Left arrow on the far left edge (slightly overlapping or just outside the cards), right arrow on the far right edge. Arrows have dark background (#1A1A1A) with cream icon, hover state changes to burgundy (#6B1A1A). Left arrow is disabled/hidden on the first slide, right arrow is disabled/hidden on the last slide. Arrows use inline SVG chevron icons (no icon library). On mobile, arrows are smaller (32px) and positioned at bottom-center as a pair instead of on the sides.

Carousel navigation — DOT INDICATORS: Row of small dots centered below the carousel. Number of dots = number of slides (ceil of filteredPosts.length / cardsPerView). Active dot is burgundy (#6B1A1A) and slightly larger (10px), inactive dots are border color (#D4CEC6, 8px). Clicking a dot navigates directly to that slide. Dots have a subtle scale transition on hover.

Carousel behavior: no infinite loop — it stops at the first and last slide. No autoplay. Swipe/drag support on touch devices using pointer events (pointerdown, pointermove, pointerup) with a threshold of 50px to trigger slide change. The carousel must feel smooth and responsive, not janky. When the last slide has fewer cards than cardsPerView (e.g. 2 remaining cards out of 3 slots), those cards should still display left-aligned with empty space on the right — do NOT stretch or center them.

Portuguese cards (type 'portuguese'): aspect-ratio 1:1, cream background (#F5F0EB), thin burgundy (#6B1A1A) accent bar on left edge (6px wide), word in Playfair Display bold (font-size scales down for long words like AUTOCONHECIMENTO via clamp), pronunciation in parentheses + classification in brackets below in DM Sans small muted text, definitions listed below in DM Sans 0.72rem, handle @psicologamarinahiga at card bottom in small muted text. Card content uses flexbox column with justify-between so handle stays at bottom.

Japanese cards (type 'japanese'): aspect-ratio 1:1, burgundy gradient background (linear-gradient 145deg from #6B1A1A to #8B2E2E), subtle diagonal stripe pattern overlay at 0.04 opacity, characters centered in large serif font (~2-3rem via clamp), romanization in brackets below in DM Sans with 0.05em letter-spacing, meaning text below in italic when present, bullet items prefixed with ✦ when bullets array exists, decorative horizontal line divider when divider:true, handle @psicologamarinahiga at card bottom in cream text at 0.5 opacity.

Hover effect on all cards: translateY(-4px) with elevated box-shadow on hover, transition duration 0.4s with cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1). No transform or shadow at rest beyond a subtle base shadow.

Project metadata footer below carousel: separated by top border in --border color. Displays client (@psicologamarinahiga), type (Social Media Design), post count (dynamic from data length). Right-aligned dark pill button 'Ver no Instagram' linking to https://instagram.com/psicologamarinahiga (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer) that changes to burgundy on hover. Metadata section uses flex-wrap for mobile stacking.

Framer Motion animations: section heading and filter tabs use whileInView fade-up. The carousel container fades in as a whole on viewport entry (no per-card stagger on initial load — the stagger effect comes naturally from the carousel sliding). All animations use viewport={{ once: true }}, ease [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0] as const, duration 0.4-0.8s. Respect useReducedMotion — when active, carousel still slides but with reduced duration (0.15s) and no other animations.

Accessibility: section element with id='social-posts' and aria-label='Social Media Design Showcase'. h2 for section heading. h3 for each card word/concept. Carousel has role='region' and aria-roledescription='carousel'. Each slide group has role='group' and aria-roledescription='slide'. Arrow buttons have aria-label ('Anterior' / 'Próximo'). Dots have aria-label ('Ir para slide N'). All interactive elements keyboard accessible. Left/right arrow keys navigate the carousel when it has focus.

Integration: import and render <SocialPosts /> in app/page.tsx — this is the ONLY modification to an existing file. Suggested position: between Projects and Skills sections, or after Experience. Single import line + single JSX line.

DO NOT modify: Navbar.tsx, Footer.tsx, Hero.tsx, About.tsx, Process.tsx, Projects.tsx, Skills.tsx, Experience.tsx, Pricing.tsx, FAQ.tsx, Contact.tsx, SectionHeading.tsx, ProjectCard.tsx, SkillBadge.tsx, ExperienceEntry.tsx, or any other existing component.
DO NOT modify: any existing data file (hero.json, trustedBy.json, about.json, process.json, projects.json, skills.json, experience.json, pricing.json, faq.json).
DO NOT modify: globals.css, tailwind.config.ts, next.config.mjs, or layout.tsx.
DO NOT add new npm dependencies — Framer Motion is already installed. The carousel is built with plain React state + CSS transforms, NOT a carousel library like Swiper or Embla.
Use inline styles or Tailwind classes for the burgundy color (#6B1A1A, #8B2E2E) rather than modifying tailwind.config.ts. The cream color #F5F0EB already exists as --bg-primary / bg-primary.

Extensibility: the component renders cards via a switch/map on post.type. Adding a new type in the future (e.g. 'quote', 'carousel', 'image') only requires: (1) adding the new type's data shape to the JSON, (2) adding a new card sub-component inside SocialPosts.tsx, (3) adding a case in the render switch. The carousel, filters, metadata, and animations don't change. Add a code comment documenting this pattern at the top of the component.

userStories.title: Certifications showcase component
userStories.description: Create a new Certifications section that displays Bianca's 9 professional certificates in an elegant, compact layout. Each certificate shows the course name, issuing institution, and an optional badge/tag for the institution. The section sits right after Experience in the page flow — forming a natural 'Experience → Certifications' narrative. The design uses a two-column masonry-style card layout on desktop (single column on mobile) with institution names as visual anchors. Like US-016, this is a NEW standalone component — do NOT modify any existing components, data files, styles, or configs.
acceptanceCriteria: PREREQUISITE: Execute the Mandatory Change Audit (Step 4 in Ralph agent instructions) before writing any code. Document any unrecorded changes in progress.txt as 'Manual Changes Audit (pre-US-017)' block. If the audit was already done for US-016 in the same session and no further manual changes occurred, note 'Audit: clean since US-016' and proceed.

New file: data/certifications.json with the following structure: { sectionLabel, heading, description, items: [...] }. Each item has: id (number), title (string), institution (string), category (string — one of 'produtividade', 'comunicação', 'marketing', 'criatividade', 'educação', 'tecnologia'). The 9 certificates are: (1) 'Estratégia de produtividade: clareza, propósito e priorização de tarefas' from ENAP, category 'produtividade'; (2) 'Happiness: Managing Happiness' from HarvardX, category 'educação'; (3) 'Comunicação não violenta' from Educaweb, category 'comunicação'; (4) 'Marketing de redes sociais – curso completo 10 em 1' from Udemy, category 'marketing'; (5) 'World Creativity Day' from World Creativity Organization, category 'criatividade'; (6) 'Relacionamento interpessoal' from Associação Brasileira de Educação a Distância, category 'comunicação'; (7) 'Marketing digital para o empreendedor' from SEBRAE, category 'marketing'; (8) 'Comunicação científica visual' from MIND, category 'comunicação'; (9) 'Avaliadora – Festival Marista de Robótica 2023' from PUCRS, category 'tecnologia'. No content hardcoded in the component.
New file: components/Certifications.tsx — a single 'use client' component. All sub-components defined inside this one file.

Section header: label 'CERTIFICATIONS' in DM Sans uppercase with wide letter-spacing (0.2em), heading 'Certificados & Formação' in Playfair Display with 'Formação' in italic, short description like 'Aprendizado contínuo em design, comunicação e estratégia.' in DM Sans --text-secondary.

Card layout: two-column grid on desktop (grid-cols-2), single column on mobile. Gap of 16px. Cards are NOT square — they are compact horizontal cards (auto height, no fixed aspect-ratio). Each card has: institution name in Playfair Display bold (font-size ~1rem) as the primary visual anchor, certificate title below in DM Sans regular (font-size 0.85rem, --text-secondary), and a small category pill/badge in the top-right corner (same badge-bg/badge-border tokens from the project: white background, subtle border, DM Sans 0.65rem uppercase). Cards have --bg-secondary (#FFFFFF) background with subtle border (--border: #D4CEC6), rounded-lg corners, padding 20-24px.

Institution emphasis: the institution name is the visual hero of each card because it conveys credibility (HarvardX, SEBRAE, ENAP, PUCRS). Use Playfair Display at readable size with --text-primary color. For long institution names (like 'Associação Brasileira de Educação a Distância'), ensure text wraps gracefully without breaking the card layout.

Category badges: each card shows its category as a small pill in the top-right. Categories use the existing badge styling (badge-bg + badge-border Tailwind tokens). Text is uppercase, 0.65rem, DM Sans. Categories are in Portuguese: 'Produtividade', 'Comunicação', 'Marketing', 'Criatividade', 'Educação', 'Tecnologia'.

Optional category filter: a row of small pill buttons above the grid (same style as the filter tabs in SocialPosts/US-016) that filter certificates by category. 'Todos' shows all 9. Since there are 6 categories for 9 items, the filter is optional — implement it only if it looks good with the layout. If implemented, active state is dark (#1A1A1A) background with cream text (not burgundy — this section uses the main site palette, not the social media burgundy). If skipped, add a code comment noting it can be added later.

Hover effect on cards: subtle border color darkening (border goes from #D4CEC6 to #1A1A1A at 0.3 opacity) and a very slight translateY(-2px) lift. Transition 0.3s ease. Keep it understated — this section should feel calm and professional, not flashy.

Framer Motion animations: cards stagger in with fade-up (staggerChildren 0.06s). Section heading fades up on viewport entry. All animations use viewport={{ once: true }}, ease [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0] as const, duration 0.5s. Respect useReducedMotion — when active, simple opacity fade only.

Accessibility: section element with id='certifications' and aria-label='Certificados e Formação'. h2 for section heading. Each card is an article element. Institution name in h3. All filter buttons (if implemented) keyboard accessible.

Integration: import and render <Certifications /> in app/page.tsx — this is the ONLY modification to an existing file. Position: immediately after the Experience section (<Experience /> then <Certifications />). Single import line + single JSX line.

DO NOT modify: any existing component file (Navbar, Footer, Hero, About, Process, Projects, SocialPosts, Skills, Experience, Pricing, FAQ, Contact, SectionHeading, ProjectCard, SkillBadge, ExperienceEntry).
DO NOT modify: any existing data file (hero.json, trustedBy.json, about.json, process.json, projects.json, socialPosts.json, skills.json, experience.json, pricing.json, faq.json).
DO NOT modify: globals.css, tailwind.config.ts, next.config.mjs, or layout.tsx.
DO NOT add new npm dependencies.

Extensibility: adding a new certificate only requires appending an object to the items array in certifications.json. No component changes needed. If a new category is added, it automatically appears in the filter (if implemented) and as a badge. Add a code comment at the top of the component documenting this.

functionalRequirements: FR-1: Built with Next.js (App Router) and TypeScript
FR-2: Styled with Tailwind CSS using warm neutral light theme (cream backgrounds, dark text, serif headings)
FR-3: All content stored in JSON files under data/ directory
FR-4: Navbar highlights active section and supports smooth scroll
FR-5: Fully responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop
FR-6: Project cards link out to live demos and/or Behance
FR-7: Contact form validates inputs client-side before showing success state
FR-8: Semantic HTML elements throughout for accessibility
FR-9: Framer Motion for scroll-driven and entrance animations; CSS transitions for hover states and micro-interactions
FR-10: Static export (output: 'export') for deployment to Vercel/Netlify/GitHub Pages
FR-11: All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion media query for accessibility
nonGoals: No GSAP or other heavy animation libraries (Framer Motion is the sole animation library)
No dark/light theme toggle — ships light only
No real payment processing for pricing plans
